rationale: The det.rs obligation asks to prove r1 == r2 for two StrictlyOrderedMap<K> values whose views are both Map::empty() and which both satisfy valid(). With the real (non-cheating) struct definitions the obligation is unprovable without forbidden axioms: StrictlyOrderedMap contains keys.v: Vec<K> and vals: Vec<ID>, and Verus's SMT equality on Vec is opaque — two empty vectors are not necessarily SMT-equal (verified by isolated experiments: `proof fn(a: Vec<u8>, b: Vec<u8>) requires a@==b@, a.len()==0, b.len()==0 ensures a==b` fails, even with all vstd broadcast lemmas including lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq / lemma_vec_obeys_deep_eq / group_vstd_default). Ghost<_> equality IS determined by view (separate test passed), and the Map view r1@==r2@ is derivable, but the two Vec fields block structural equality. I derived the maximum honest content: revealed valid/map_valid/view, proved keys@/vals@ are empty Seqs (via to_set==empty), proved view-level equalities r1.keys@==r2.keys@, r1.vals@==r2.vals@,
